zh.lifeSummary: Thomas de Hulme是Adam de Hulme的兒子，於大約1230年出生。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Thomas de Hulme是Adam de Hulme的儿子，于大约1230年出生。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hulme
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Holm   and of   Home   3 and 4.

variants.name: Hume
meanings: Scottish (Lowlands) and northern English: variant of Hulme , with loss of l before the bilabial consonant /m/, as in the vocabulary word calm. See Holm and Home .

Scottish: habitational name either from the barony of Home or from Hume (or Home) in Berwickshire, from which the barony took its name. The placename derives from Old English hōum, dative plural form of hōh ‘heel, spur of land’.
History: A family of this name (see 2 above) have held the Earldom of Home since 1605.

variants.name: Holm
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Anders, Thor, Holger, Nels, Nils, Sven, Helmer, Lennart, Niels, Vibeke.

English, Scottish, German, and Scandinavian: topographic name for someone who lived on an island, in particular a piece of slightly raised land lying in a fen or partly surrounded by streams, from Middle English, Middle Low German holm, Old Norse holmr, or else a habitational name from a place called with this element. The Swedish name is often ornamental.

English: topographic name for someone who lived where holly grew, from Middle English holm, a divergent development of Old English hole(g)n ‘holly (tree)’; or, especially in southern England, a habitational name from any of the places such as East and West Holme (Dorset) or Holne (Devon). Compare Hollis .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas de Hulme was born about 1230 as the son of Adam de Hulme. He had at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Thomas de Hulme est né environ 1230, fils de Adam de Hulme. Il a eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Thomas de Hulme wurde um 1230, als Sohn von Adam de Hulme geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Thomas de Hulme è nato il circa 1230 come figlio di Adam de Hulme. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas de Hulmeは約1230年にAdam de Hulmeの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas de Hulme(은)는 대략 1230년 Adam de Hulme의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Thomas de Hulme nasceu aproximadamente 1230 filho de Adam de Hulme. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Thomas de Hulme родился около 1230 г. Его отца звали Adam de Hulme. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Thomas de Hulme nació aproximadamente 1230 como hijo de Adam de Hulme. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
